mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household consumption and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate profit growth expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistically significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods, or the joint signal produces negative returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising CPIAUCSL combined with a rising FEDFUNDS compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 65 percent of instances where both series rise simultaneously over a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate cash flows scale with economic output.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds the historical median, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 150,000 jobs signals robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending power, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences across a 4-year walk-forward validation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential investment alongside falling consumer price inflation reduces borrowing costs in real terms while boosting construction sector earnings, jointly generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns fail to outperform the baseline by at least 0.5 percent when both series move in the specified directions, or the interaction term shows zero predictive power in a logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a rising industrial production index paired with a declining unemployment rate confirms supply-side normalization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances during identified recovery periods, or the cross-sectional Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year window.
